description_long: This object consists of three irregularly shaped gray rocks, stacked vertically in a balanced arrangement. The rocks have a rough, weathered surface with visible textural details and subtle variations in shading, suggesting natural stone. The edges are rounded and uneven, with each rock differing slightly in size and form, giving the stack a natural, unmanicured appearance. The coloration is predominantly a medium to light gray, with some darker patches and mottling that enhance the realistic stone look. The rocks appear solid and heavy, with no visible cavities or artificial modifications. This arrangement is typical of decorative or functional rock groupings found in urban parks or landscaped areas.
description: A stack of three irregular, gray, rough-textured rocks with natural, weathered surfaces and rounded edges.
description_view_0: Three rocks are seen side by side, highlighting their individual shapes and rough textures.
description_view_1: The rocks are stacked vertically, showing their balanced arrangement and the varying sizes from top to bottom.
description_view_2: A side view of the three rocks, again separated, emphasizing their irregular forms and surface details.
description_view_3: A vertical stack of the rocks, similar to view 1, but from the opposite side, showing the largest rock at the base.
attribute_location: urban park or landscaped public space
